mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day SPX returns occur in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, invalidating the negative predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which amplifies corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term predictive IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in over 70% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, providing a fundamental floor that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% indicates a sudden supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, which rapidly erodes equity valuations and causes negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of severe INDPRO drawdown events, or the threshold signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward validation.
